Scenario.description: Find an already-running Chrome instance or launch a new one. Sets {{tabId}} for subsequent steps.

1. LISTBROWSERS → look for Chrome in the list. 2. If a Chrome instance is found: note the first available tab id as {{tabId}}. 3. If Chrome is NOT found: LAUNCH target="chrome" → Chrome opens with a blank tab. Capture the PAGE:id from the NEWPAGE response or LISTBROWSERS after launch. After intro, {{tabId}} is a valid tab that can receive NAVIGATE/READ/etc.

step.note: list running browser instances → select Chrome tab → assign {{tabId}}

Scenario.description: Launch Chrome (opens a new blank tab).
Use when Chrome is definitively not running. LAUNCH opens Chrome with the default new-tab page. Follow with NEWPAGE or NAVIGATE on the returned tab id.

step.note: launches Chrome; returns tab list with at least one blank tab

Scenario.description: Open a new blank tab. Returns PAGE:id as {{tabId}}.
NEWPAGE opens a blank tab (about:newtab). Reassign {{tabId}} from the response before continuing. Preferred over Ctrl+T keyboard shortcut for reliable tab id tracking.

step.note: opens blank tab; capture returned PAGE:id as {{tabId}}

Scenario.description: Navigate tab {{tabId}} to {{url}}.

Uses NAVIGATE command — cleaner and more reliable than Ctrl+L + type + Enter. After NAVIGATE, the tab loads the page; allow a brief wait before READ/QUERYTREE. The response includes the final URL (after redirects) and page title.

step.note: navigate to URL; waits for page load event

Scenario.description: Read the full visible text of the page (body.innerText).

READ with no parameter returns body.innerText — all visible text content. Useful for: verifying navigation succeeded, extracting plain-text data, checking for error messages, reading search results. For structured data prefer EXECUTE to return JSON. Does NOT return HTML markup.
Read the text content or value of element {{sel}}.
Focus the input element {{sel}} and type {{text}}.

Uses SENDKEYS with "css:{{sel}}|{{text}}" — this focuses the element then types. Use for: search boxes, login fields, form inputs. To clear first: EXECUTE "document.querySelector('{{sel}}').value=''" then fill. To submit after typing: append {ENTER} to {{text}} or use scenario "submit-form".
Click the element identified by CSS selector {{sel}}.
Fill text field {{sel}} with {{text}} and submit by pressing Enter.

Combines fill-input + {ENTER} key to submit. For multi-field forms: call fill-input for each field, then click the submit button. Monitor URL or page text change after submit to detect navigation/response.

step.note: type into field and press Enter to submit

Scenario.description: Execute JavaScript {{jsCode}} in the page and return the result.

EXECUTE runs synchronous JS; returns JSON-serialized result. For async operations: wrap in an IIFE with setTimeout or use await in a (async () => { ... })() pattern if Chrome supports top-level await. Useful for: extracting structured data, checking element states, scrolling, triggering events, reading cookies/storage, injecting UI interactions. Example: "JSON.stringify(Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(a=>a.href))"

step.note: run JS in page context; returns serialized result

Scenario.description: Capture a full-page screenshot of tab {{tabId}}.
SCREENSHOT returns a base64-encoded PNG. Use for: visual verification, debugging, capturing form states. The result can be decoded and saved to disk via EXECUTE or external processing.

step.note: returns base64 PNG of current tab viewport

Scenario.description: Get the DOM accessibility tree of the current page.

QUERYTREE via CDP returns an accessibility snapshot of the page. Useful for: discovering element roles and names, finding selectors, mapping out interactive elements. Result is a JSON/XML tree; interpret roles and names to identify elements. Depth may be limited — use EXECUTE for deep inspection of specific subtrees.
Scroll to the bottom of the page (to load lazy content).
Leave the tab open. No teardown.
No-op teardown. Use when you want the user to see the page.
Close the tab that was used for automation.
Navigate to about:blank (clear page state without closing tab).

step.note: navigates to blank page; tab remains open
